meaning: adj.古怪的,引人发笑的,嘲弄的,探询的
examples: He gave Robin a mildly quizzical glance.
Smirks, raised eyebrows, and quizzical looks are not out of the realm of possibility.
"He said, something quizzical in his eyes."
Alabama and the SEC are in a league of their own, despite a few quizzical bowl losses.
Ask most people - even Londoners - what that means and I suspect you will get quizzical looks.
